events.raw_text:

Wed, Jun 18, 2025 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM EDT

Klaviyo

125 Summer Street Floor 7 · Boston, MA

dbt Global Circuit Series: dbt Boston Meetup - CY25- June

Hosted by Chitra S. and 2 others

Boston dbt Meetup
4.8
70 reviews
Details

What are dbt meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.
Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.

This meetup is extra special because we have dbt Labs participating as speakers. In this meetup, they’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas they’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why they built them the way they did.

🤝Organizers: Chitra Sundaram (Cleartelligence), Riddhima Shukla (Cleartelligence), Stefan Mitrano (Cleartelligence)
🏠Venue Host: 125 Summer Street, Boston 02110

🍕Catering: TBD
To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy
📝Agenda

6:00 - 7:00 | Check in/Registration/ Networking (60 min)
7:00 - 7:05 | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
7:05 - 7:25 | Presentation 1: Raw to Ready: How dbt Powers the AI-Ready Data Lake (15-20 min)
7:25 - 7:30 | Presentation 1 Q&A (5 min)
7:30 - 7:50 | Presentation 2: For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs (15-20 min)
7:50 - 7:55 | Presentation 2 Q&A (5 min)
7:55 - 8:00 | Closing Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)

🗣️Presentation #1: Raw to Ready: How dbt Powers the AI-Ready Data Lake
Speaker: Kanishk Mittal, Lead Technical Specialist, InterSystems
---
🗣️Presentation #2 For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs
Speakers: Grace Goheen, Product Manager and Jeremy Cohen, Principal Product Manager, dbt Labs

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-boston channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 50,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

dbt Meetups are gatherings dedicated to helping you own your analytics engineering workflow. Events will focus on the impact of dbt in the data community and professional development tools for analytics engineers.
Expect big-picture conversations related to the data stacks, data operations, data teams, modeling, testing, dashboards, etc. If you work with data, this group is for you! We welcome data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more!! While students are welcome, this event is more geared toward professionals.
Read more

events.page_title: Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs), Thu, Nov 21, 2024, 2:00 PM | Meetup
events.raw_text:
Skip to content

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Boston dbt Meetup
4.8
70 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!

Hosted by Boston dbt Meetup

Boston dbt Meetup
4.8

Details

Join the data engineering community on September 27 and hear two more stories of end-user experiences with dbt. We will predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, but will also discuss broader topics related to data teams, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

Come hang out with some awesome data practitioners, have a bite to eat, and hear great presentations

Capturing History in dbt
Daniel Allen, Lead Data Engineer, Rapid7

Our story of dynamically capturing history on Snowflake tables through our dbt models Summary: Capturing history is a common and valuable practice within data engineering. It is useful for understanding the lifecycle of your data, for auditing changes, and to see point-in-time snapshots of datasets. Using Snowflake and dbt, I will talk through how we implemented dynamic history capture that can be easily applied to any model within dbt. I'll discuss the design and the technical implementation of our history capture process along with the benefits and limitations of our approach.

Talk Title TBA
Jason Hall, Senior Solutions Architect, Upsolver

A big thank you to our friends at Rapid 7 for hosting this event!

events.raw_text:

Wed, Jun 14, 2023 · 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM EDT

1 Lincoln St

1 Lincoln St · Boston, MA

How to find us
Boston dbt Meetup - Q2 Edition!
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Boston dbt Meetup
4.8
70 reviews
Details

Join the data engineering community on June 14th and hear two more stories of end-user experiences with dbt. We will predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, but will also discuss broader topics related to data teams, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

A big thank you to our friends at Upsolver for sponsoring this event!

Presentations Include:

Empower Datamesh with dbt: Streamlining workflows and promoting cross-team collaboration
William Kuan, Senior Data Engineer, Rapid7

I'll explore the benefits of using dbt in a decentralized approach to managing analytical data in complex and large-scale environments. By distributing data responsibility to those closest to it, this approach encourages agility, innovation, and accountability. With a decentralized platform in dbt, teams can collaborate, self-serve, and work in parallel, leading to more efficient achievement of data-driven goals. This approach empowers teams to take ownership of their data and work with it in a way that suits them, while also facilitating cross-team collaboration and simplifying CICD and workflows.

Using dbt to optimize queries from your phone
Sabin Thomas, Founder & CTO, Zing Data

Data analysis tools have traditionally been built for desktops and for power users. The team at Zing Data believes in data interactivity that has been built for the masses - that is mobile-first, simple, collaborative and ubiquitous. In this session we'll go over architecture patterns for this next-gen of tooling, and the difference in needs for emerging market users for data products. I will also examine using dbt to evaluate SQL query composition patterns and how Zing Data has evolved these patterns to account for mobile first problems - poor connectivity, long running queries, push notifications, location-sensitive query composition and others.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-boston channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

Join the data engineering community for the first dbt Boston Meetup of 2023! Presentations include:

How to Build Reports from Raw Google Analytics 4 Data using dbt
Adam Ribaudo, VP of Data Activation, Velir

Adam Ribaudo is the VP of Data Activation at Velir and is passionate about the intersection of marketing technology and data. He's a seasoned digital marketing consultant of 17 years who started as a web developer and has since made a career out of synthesizing technical concepts into actionable advice for business leaders. In his work, he provides his teams and clients with the tools and processes necessary to activate data and turn it into business value.

Learning to Love dbt
Kasey Mazza, Analytics Engineering Manager, RevOps, HubSpot

Kasey wasn't always a dbt true believer. In her talk, she will discuss how she learned to love dbt through her implementation experience at HubSpot.

Come hang out with us! Hear some cool talks, network with an amazing community, and grab a bite to eat while you're at it.

Please note that by attending, you consent to Cleartelligence sharing (1) records of your image, voice, and participation in discussions/talks/networking (for the purpose of facilitating remote attendance, participation, and education for participants with health, travel or other constraints), and (2) your registration information for purposes of planning for, hosting, administering meetups and in-person events, and communicating about Cleartelligence and its events.

Details

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.
✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Review the schedule here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com/schedule
⭐As a member of the dbt Meetup Network, please use your discount code MEETUP15 for Premium and GA tickets.

Details

🦆 We are excited to help bring Boston-based folks in the dbt Community together! Looking forward to socializing and seeing both new and familiar faces.

🙏 Please use you first & last name when RSVPing as the venue will cross-check Meetup.com registration names for this event with attendee identifications.🙏

✅Agenda and additional details below:
✨ 6:30pm Intro Remarks with Emily Hawkins | Refreshments & Conversation (coffee, tea, refreshments & snacks)
📍Spread across two spaces, The Forum & Nourish (The Gallery)
✨ 7:00-9:00pm Reception (more socializing, light food and refreshments ☕🍺🍷🥤🧀)
📍The Gallery
✨ 9:00pm (or earlier, depending on attendance) Thank you remarks by Emily Hawkins
We have the space until 9:30, so you are welcome to finish up your tea, drinks, or snacks until then!

✰ Thank you Starburst Data for helping us spread the word in Boston! 📣

🤝🏽 For updates, join the conversation in the #local-bos channel in dbt Slack! This is where the chat will also take place during meetups.
Join the dbt Slack community:
https://www.getdbt.com/community/

COVID-19 Policy:
Any public space where other people are present holds an inherent risk of exposure to COVID-19 and other communicable diseases. By attending this event, I agree to voluntarily assume all risk related to exposure and agree to not hold dbt Labs or any of their affiliates including partners and sponsors, directors, officers, employees, agents, contractors, volunteers (“Representatives”) or venues liable for illness. I understand and agree that dbt Labs has an interest and obligation in protecting the health and well-being of dbt Labs Representatives, venue staff and other attendees; therefore, I agree that, if I’ve chosen not to be fully vaccinated as determined by my local country’s public health guidelines for personal, religious, medical or other reasons, I will only participate in a dbt Labs meetup remotely by watching live or via a recording, and I will not attend in-person. When in attendance in person, I will take necessary precautions while at the event in accordance with local and national health guidelines and regulations, which may include, but be not limited to, engaging in appropriate social distancing, wearing a mask in public areas when not consuming food or beverages, minimizing face touching, frequently washing hands and avoiding risky environments such as overcrowded bars or restaurants. I agree to not attend any dbt Labs event if I feel ill or have had recent exposure to a potentially COVID-19 infected individual. Venue may have other covid- or public health-related requirements in place, and I agree to abide by those requirements when disclosed by venue.

Wed, May 26, 2021 · 6:00 PM to 7:15 PM CEST

Online event

This event has passed

Boston dbt Meetup online

Hosted by Fatima C.

Boston dbt Meetup
4.8
70 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are gatherings dedicated to helping you own your analytics engineering workflow. Events will focus on the impact of dbt (data build tool) in the data community and professional development tools for analytics engineers.

Expect big-picture conversations related to the data stacks, data operations, data teams, modeling, testing, dashboards, etc.

If you work with data, this group is for you! We welcome data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more!!

-----------------------------------

Join the conversation in the #local-bos channel in dbt Slack! This is where the chat will take place during meetups.

Receive an invite to join the dbt Slack community:
https://community.getdbt.com/

-----------------------------------

✨ Speaker Lineup✨

👀 Evaluating Marketplace Dynamics with Geospatial Analytics
🎤 Athena Casarotto, Associate BI Manager, Drizly
🎤 Jake Smart, Production DS Manager, Drizly

Find out how Drizly, the leader in the alcohol delivery space, has built a geospatial engine – using dbt, Mapbox, and Uber's h3 – that can be visualized in Looker. Geospatial data transforms aggregated market performance into actionable insights. Learn how Drizly maps and views network capacity at the hyperlocal level.

👀 Return of the Hypercube
🎤 Alex Hancock, Head of Customer Analytics, Simply Business

Simply Business is an online insurance broker and has over 750,000 micro-business and landlord customers, covering over 1,000 types of businesses. In this session, Alex will cover their approach in building performant data models using dbt.

Alex leads the Customer Analytics team and is a part of the global Data and Analytics team at Simply Business. They are all united by a passion for building technology that enables a world-class commercial insurance experience.

🎤 This meetup will be hosted by Jillian Corkin, Community/Dev Relations at Fishtown Analytics.

-----------------------------------

✅ Agenda (EDT)

12:00 pm - 12:05 pm: Welcome Remarks by Jillian
12:05 pm - 12:25 pm: Evaluating Marketplace Dynamics with Geospatial Analytics, Athena & Jake
12:25 pm - 12:35 pm: Audience Q&A with Athena and Jake
12:35 pm - 12:55 pm: Return of the Hypercube, Alex
12:55 pm - 1:10 pm: Q&A with Alex
1:10 pm - 1:15 pm: Closing Remarks by Jillian

-----------------------------------

dbt is a command-line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

events.raw_text:

Fri, May 8, 2020 · 5:00 PM to 6:00 PM CEST

Online event

This event has passed

The first Boston dbt Meetup (online)

Hosted by janessa l. and Claire C.

Boston dbt Meetup
4.8
70 reviews
Details

The dbt community in Boston is growing and we are thrilled to be organizing the first Meetup! While we won't be able to gather in-person, we are looking forward to hearing from two fantastic speakers, and getting to meet some of the Boston data teams currently implementing dbt.

For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #events-bos channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------------
Speaker Lineup

Designing more informative data models with dbt
Ashley Sherwood, Senior Data Analyst
📣HubSpot
Data Stack: Snowflake, dbt, Airflow, Looker

HubSpot’s partner program includes hundreds of solution resellers around the world, and represents a significant portion of the company’s revenue. Ashley identified a gap in the team’s ability to report on the engagement of this valuable segment then built a new table to solve this gap. Learn how she designed the data model in dbt, and collaborated with business users to leverage the new insights this model delivered for 2020 annual planning.

Bridging the "GAAP" between Finance and BI with dbt
Christopher Bertrand, Data Scientist
📹Wistia
Data Stack: Fivetran, Redshift, dbt, Mode

Modelling revenue was Wistia's first large-scale dbt project. Three years on, this work continues to pay dividends by providing insights across the business. In this talk, Chris will walk through the history of this project, highlighting the key improvements made to increase the usability and accuracy of the financial data set.

----------------------------------------
dbt speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.
